sprint: Change Look Modal — Oversized Representation Thumbnails Fixed
task: A direct, user-reported layout bug: the mid-edit 'Change Look' modal (Context Panel's Change Representation shortcut) rendered a giant, cropped photo overflowing its panel with visible scrollbars, distinct from the earlier 'fills the entire screen' bug already fixed via the mode-change-look CSS class. Root cause: js/creationFlow.js's _repCard() renders an whenever a Representation's thumbnail resolves to a real image (commonly the World's own Hero Image, via _repThumbnail()'s fallback chain), but css/style.css had zero rules for that class -- the image rendered at its full intrinsic photo resolution with nothing constraining its size, blowing out its card, the grid, and the mode-change-look panel's own 560px/85vh box. Confirmed via a real git-stash A/B test: pre-fix the panel's scrollWidth (1211px) genuinely exceeded its 560px clientWidth with each image rendering at its full 900x1200 natural size; post-fix both images constrain to 196x110 with zero overflow. Fixed with one new CSS rule matching the same width:100%/fixed-height/object-fit:cover shape every other Creation Flow image thumbnail already uses. No JS/architecture change. Verified via a new Playwright reproduction plus full regression across the entire existing Creator/Builder/Card-Platform Playwright suite and both goldenBuild.js suites, all passing unchanged. See CLAUDE.md's matching entry for the full account.
